description: Tala is Auckland's most celebrated restaurant, helmed by chef Henry Onesemo who was born and raised in Samoa. Set in a warmly designed intimate space in Parnell — formerly home to the legendary Pasture — the experience centres on two immersive tasting journeys that weave Samoan culinary heritage, the umu open fire, and a traditional hand-washing ceremony into every detail. Named 2025 Supreme Winner at the Viva Top 50 and holder of three Cuisine Good Food Award hats, Tala has put Samoan cuisine on the global fine-dining map.
highlights: Three-hat Samoan fine dining tasting menu in Parnell; umu open fire cooking; intimate chef's counter; Viva Supreme Winner 2025; Best Chef Awards knife in Milan.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; Viva Top 50 2025; Best Chef Awards Milan; RNZ; NZ Herald

description: Ahi — meaning fire in Te Reo Maori — is chef Ben Bayly's landmark showcase of New Zealand produce, occupying a light-filled dining room on Level 2 of Commercial Bay with sparkling Waitemata Harbour views. The tasting menus, built around an organic kitchen garden in South Auckland and the finest seasonal ingredients from named growers across Aotearoa, are both artfully presented and deeply flavour-driven. Restaurant of the Year at the 2024 Cuisine Good Food Awards and a World's 50 Best Discovery listing, Ahi is the definitive expression of modern New Zealand fine dining.
highlights: Three-hat NZ fine dining at Commercial Bay waterfront; World's 50 Best Discovery; organic kitchen garden produce; harbour views; Cuisine Restaurant of the Year 2024.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; World's 50 Best Discovery; Time Out Auckland; Viva Top 50; NZ Herald

description: Paris Butter is a sophisticated neighbourhood fine diner tucked into a sleek, frosted-windowed space on Herne Bay's Jervois Road. Co-owners chefs Nick Honeyman — whose sister restaurant Le Petit Leon in France holds a Michelin star — and Zennon Wijlens, NZ Chef of the Year 2023-2024, craft evolving degustation menus of three, six or eight courses that draw on personal memories and seasonal NZ produce. Named Metro Best Fine Dining Restaurant 2025 and a three-hat laureate, Paris Butter is considered Auckland's most likely contender for a Michelin star.
highlights: Three-hat modern NZ fine dining; Michelin-adjacent pedigree; seasonal tasting menus; intimate design-led room; NZ Herald and Metro top rankings.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; Metro Restaurant of the Year 2025; Viva Top 50 2025; NZ Herald; Broadsheet

description: Cocoro — meaning heart and soul in Japanese — is Auckland's pre-eminent Japanese degustation restaurant, tucked discreetly behind a bamboo wall on a side street off Ponsonby Road. Chef-owner Makoto Tokuyama, raised in a Zen Buddhist temple in Japan, delivers meticulous multi-course seafood and omakase menus built around the philosophy of ichigo ichie — treasuring the unrepeatable nature of each moment. With three Cuisine hats, a World's 50 Best Discovery listing, and a legendary 25-species sashimi platter, Cocoro is a benchmark institution in its category.
highlights: Three-hat Japanese degustation; World's 50 Best Discovery; 25-species sashimi platter; intimate Ponsonby setting; chef Makoto Tokuyama's Zen heritage.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; World's 50 Best Discovery; Metro Magazine; NZ Herald; Broadsheet

description: Kazuya is Auckland's most rarefied fine-dining experience, a serene and intimate 25-seat room on Symonds Street where chef-owner Kazuya Yamauchi presents formal European-Japanese tasting menus with white-gloved precision. His renowned Texture dish — 30 different vegetables cut into perfect cubes — exemplifies the extraordinary level of craft at work here, while Japanese whisky and sake selections provide an exquisite accompaniment. Equal parts gallery and dining room, Kazuya delivers one of the most memorable and singular fine-dining experiences in the Southern Hemisphere.
highlights: Intimate European-Japanese degustation; white tablecloth precision; famous Texture dish of 30 vegetables; premium whisky and sake programme; 5, 7 or 9-course menus.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards; Metro Magazine; Viva Top 60; The Denizen; Time Out Auckland

description: Onslow is chef Josh Emett's masterwork, a warm and sophisticated dining room occupying the ground floor of The International on Princes Street, steps from Albert Park and the Auckland Art Gallery. Emett's 30-year career spanning Gordon Ramsay's empire in London, New York and Melbourne is distilled into an elegant a la carte and table d'hote menu of premium New Zealand produce — including a signature side-of-salmon trolley carved tableside. The richly textured interior with dark timber, stone floors and gnarled indoor trees creates a mood of old-world luxury.
highlights: Chef Josh Emett's signature restaurant; landmark CBD address; salmon trolley service; private garden terrace with Sky Tower views; premium provenance-led menu.
sources: Metro Restaurant of the Year 2024; Viva Top 50; Broadsheet; NZ Herald; Cuisine Good Food Guide

description: Advieh is the hotel restaurant of the InterContinental Auckland at One Queen Street, delivering one of the city's most exciting dining propositions under acclaimed chef Gareth Stewart. Named after a Persian spice blend, the restaurant weaves Middle Eastern and Mediterranean flavours through an inventive menu of locally sourced New Zealand produce, with harbour views as the backdrop. Hotel Restaurant of the Year at the 2024 Cuisine Good Food Awards, Advieh impresses with its theatrical whole dry-aged duck, chicken-liver baklava, and an energetic bar programme.
highlights: Hotel Restaurant of the Year 2024; Cuisine one-hat; Middle Eastern-Mediterranean fine dining; harbour views; Wagyu tableside carving; InterContinental waterfront setting.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2024/2025; NZ Herald; The Denizen; Auckland NZ Official; Heart of the City

description: Cassia is widely recognised as New Zealand's finest modern Indian restaurant after over a decade of critically acclaimed dining in Auckland's CBD under chef Sid Sahrawat. Currently transitioning to a new standalone address on Albert and Wyndham Streets opening August 2026, the restaurant's two-hat Cuisine pedigree, celebrated Journey through India tasting menus, and signature dishes like roasted carrot with dry korma remain as compelling as ever. Sahrawat's creative command of spice, local produce and classical Indian technique places Cassia in a global conversation.
highlights: Two-hat modern Indian fine dining; Cuisine Good Food Award winner; Journey through India tasting menu; acclaimed chef Sid Sahrawat; CBD relocation to new address August 2026.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; NZ Herald; Broadsheet

description: Sidart occupies the first floor of Three Lamps Plaza on Ponsonby Road, offering some of the finest views of the Auckland city skyline from its intimate, warmly lit dining room. Chef Lesley Chandra — who inherited the restaurant from its celebrated founder Sid Sahrawat in 2021 — brings a Fijian-Indian sensibility to elevated bistro menus that remain technically brilliant and full of surprise. A regular fixture in Metro and Viva top restaurant lists, Sidart continues to stand as one of Auckland's defining fine-dining addresses.
highlights: Skyline views over Auckland from Ponsonby; tasting menu fine dining; chef Lesley Chandra's Fijian-Indian influence; intimate 35-seat room; iconic Auckland fine dining address.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Guide; Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Time Out Auckland; NZ Herald

description: SOUL Bar & Bistro occupies the most coveted waterfront terrace in Auckland, set in the heart of the Viaduct Harbour with west-facing views of super-yachts, America's Cup vessels and spectacular sunsets. The restaurant delivers a confidently executed menu of the finest seasonal New Zealand seafood and produce to a loyal following of well-heeled Aucklanders, business travellers and visiting internationals. With a DJ programme, all-weather terrace and seamless all-day service, SOUL is the city's quintessential luxury waterfront dining institution.
highlights: Auckland's most iconic waterfront terrace; Viaduct Harbour setting; premium seafood focus; Bluff oyster and crayfish seasons; DJ evenings; perennial top-50 restaurant.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Heart of the City; NZ Herald

description: Gemmayze Street is the Supreme Winner of the Viva Top 60 Auckland Restaurants 2024, a celebrated Lebanese restaurant by chef Samir Allen set at the far end of the heritage St Kevin's Arcade on Karangahape Road. The menu of Lebanese mezze, exquisite tasting menus and legendary hummus is delivered in a room framed by wrought-iron bay windows overlooking Myers Park and the city skyline, with craft cocktails completing the picture. With its warm, generous hospitality and outstanding culinary credibility, Gemmayze Street is one of Auckland's most beloved dining destinations.
highlights: Viva Top 60 Supreme Winner 2024; iconic St Kevin's Arcade setting; celebrated Lebanese mezze; legendary hummus; city skyline views; craft cocktail programme.
sources: Viva Top 60 2024; Metro Top 50; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Broadsheet; Time Out Auckland

description: Gilt Brasserie is Josh and Helen Emett's grand all-day dining destination, housed in the beautifully restored 1924 Chancery Chambers building at the corner of Chancery and O'Connell Streets in Auckland's CBD. With leather-topped tables, velvet seating and bespoke interior detailing by Knight Associates, the 130-seat room invokes the spirit of New York's Balthazar for Auckland's downtown. The menu of deceptively simple yet masterfully executed European fare — signature King crab pappardelle, duck frites, impeccable baked oysters — makes this the city's finest grand brasserie.
highlights: Auckland's premier grand brasserie; heritage 1924 Chancery Chambers; Josh Emett's European menu; all-day service; 130-seat luxurious room; acclaimed wine and cocktail programme.
sources: Metro Top 50 2024; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Broadsheet; Concrete Playground

description: Tantalus Estate is Waiheke Island's most celebrated dining destination, a family-owned premium vineyard set in the heart of the Onetangi Valley on 20 acres of vines, olive groves and native wetlands. The elegant restaurant — framed by botanical courtyards and sweeping vineyard views — is led by Head Chef Axel Curtet-Latreille, whose French-trained sensibility and respect for organically grown local produce result in menus of exceptional refinement. Just 35 minutes by ferry from downtown Auckland, a long lunch or Trust the Chef dinner at Tantalus is one of the region's quintessential luxury experiences.
highlights: Premium vineyard restaurant on Waiheke Island; French-influenced seasonal menus; botanical courtyards and vineyard views; Trust the Chef tasting menus; Metro Best Destination Restaurant.
sources: Metro Restaurant of the Year; Cuisine Good Food Awards; Viva Top 50; Auckland NZ Official; Time Out Auckland

description: Metita is the masterwork of Michael Meredith, one of New Zealand's most celebrated chefs, a Pacific restaurant within The Grand by SkyCity on Federal Street that redefines what Pacific cuisine can mean in a fine-dining context. Named after Meredith's late mother, the menu draws on his Samoan heritage with dishes of smoked eel panikeke, lamb ribs with coconut and king salmon with betel leaf — all elevated to high technique without losing their soul. Hotel Restaurant of the Year at the 2025 Cuisine Good Food Awards, Metita is a landmark addition to Auckland's dining landscape.
highlights: Michael Meredith's Pacific fine dining; Hotel Restaurant of the Year 2025; SkyCity Federal Street location; Samoan heritage menus; innovative contemporary Pacific dishes.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Awards 2025; Metro Top 50; Viva Top 50; Auckland NZ Official; Urban List

description: The Oyster Inn is a cherished Waiheke Island institution, a beachside bistro and boutique hotel set in the heart of Oneroa village with an ocean-view verandah that captures everything romantic about island dining. Owned by Josh and Helen Emett since 2020, the restaurant delivers a seasonal menu of coastal classics — including the island's finest oysters, fresh market fish and indulgent mac 'n' cheese — with a laid-back luxury that makes every table feel like a private discovery. The combination of brilliant food, boutique rooms and a genuine island pace makes this one of New Zealand's most loved restaurants.
highlights: Beachside bistro on Waiheke Island; ocean-view verandah; Josh and Helen Emett ownership; Oneroa village setting; boutique hotel rooms; seasonal NZ coastal menu.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Broadsheet; Time Out Auckland; Auckland NZ Official

description: Baduzzi is a New York-Italian dining institution in Auckland's Wynyard Quarter, named after the Sicilian word for meatballs and designed by Paul Izzard in a warm, tiled room that evokes the spirit of a downtown Manhattan trattoria. The menu — venison polpette, satiny handmade pastas, wood-fired seafood — is executed with the discipline of a high-end kitchen but delivered with the generosity and buzz of a neighbourhood favourite. A perennial Metro and Viva top-50 restaurant with an extensive Italian wine list, Baduzzi attracts a loyal and discerning dining crowd.
highlights: New York-Italian landmark; Wynyard Quarter waterfront precinct; venison meatballs and handmade pasta; Sicilian-tiled Paul Izzard interior; extensive Italian wine list; perennial top-50.
sources: Metro Top 50 multiple years; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Heart of the City; Broadsheet

description: Alma is Britomart's most celebrated all-day dining room, a light-filled, beautifully designed Andalusian-inspired restaurant at the corner of Gore and Tyler Streets from the Savor Group. The menu of flame-kissed small plates draws on the flavours of southern Spain with bright produce, bold technique and seasonal surprise — a sexy evening destination with impeccable service by day and night. Cited among Auckland's best new restaurants since opening and a staple of every top-50 list, Alma has swiftly become a landmark of contemporary dining in the city.
highlights: Award-winning Andalusian dining in Britomart; flame-kissed small plates; acclaimed Savor Group; design-led fit-out; exceptional service; top-50 regular.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Concrete Playground; Broadsheet

description: Amano is the Savor Group's flagship all-day Italian restaurant in Auckland's Britomart, occupying a beautiful industrial-chic warehouse space with an open kitchen, harbour-view courtyard and one of the city's most buzzing dining rooms. The menu spans house-milled pastas, burrata, wood-fired proteins and impeccable Wagyu with a discipline and generosity that matches the best Italian restaurants in Sydney or Melbourne. A perennial Auckland top-10 restaurant with an exceptional wine list and cocktail bar, Amano draws a smart, stylish crowd day and night.
highlights: All-day Italian landmark in Britomart; open kitchen; harbour-view courtyard; house-milled pasta; exceptional wine and cocktail programme; consistently top-10 in Auckland.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Concrete Playground; Time Out Auckland

description: Sid at The French Cafe is one of Auckland's most storied fine-dining addresses, a landmark Eden Terrace restaurant that has upheld a benchmark of culinary excellence for decades under chef Sid Sahrawat and Chand Sahrawat. The elegant dining room, framed by heritage architecture and a lush garden courtyard, delivers seasonal contemporary New Zealand menus informed by Indian and European technique with precision and warmth. With an award-winning wine programme and impeccable service, it remains the choice of discerning diners celebrating life's most important occasions.
highlights: Auckland's most storied fine-dining address; heritage garden courtyard; Sid Sahrawat's flagship; award-winning wine programme; seasonal NZ menus; special-occasion institution.
sources: Cuisine Good Food Guide multiple years; Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; NZ Herald; Auckland NZ Official

description: Mr Morris is the Britomart restaurant of the highly regarded Michael Meredith, a beautifully simple contemporary space where modern Pacific and New Zealand flavours are expressed with precision, generosity and warmth. The open kitchen and striking bar anchor a room that attracts a discerning business and social crowd, while the wine list and beverage programme are among the best in the precinct. Consistently ranked in Metro and Viva top-50 lists, Mr Morris is the accessible, social complement to Meredith's more immersive work at Metita.
highlights: Michael Meredith's elegant Britomart restaurant; modern Pacific-NZ flavours; striking design; acclaimed wine programme; top-50 regular; open kitchen theatre.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Time Out Auckland; Urban List

description: Ortolana is a beautifully considered modern Italian restaurant in Auckland's Britomart, named for the Italian vegetable garden and devoted to exceptional seasonal produce from local growers and farmers. The restaurant occupies a light-filled industrial space adjacent to the Hotel Britomart, with an open kitchen and a confident vegetable-forward menu that places produce at the absolute centre of every dish. Consistently cited among the city's top tables, Ortolana draws an affluent, internationally minded crowd who appreciate its elegant simplicity and superb wine programme.
highlights: Produce-driven modern Italian in Britomart; Hotel Britomart setting; vegetable-forward menus; exceptional wine programme; consistently top-50 restaurant.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Broadsheet; Heart of the City

description: Azabu Ponsonby is Auckland's most glamorous Japanese-Peruvian fusion restaurant, from the Savor Group, occupying a sleek and intimate dining room on Ponsonby Road that feels transplanted from Sydney's Surry Hills or Tokyo's Azabu-Juban district. The Nikkei menu of ceviche, tiradito, karaage and wagyu yakitori is perfectly balanced between delicate Japanese technique and the citrus-laced energy of Peruvian cuisine, complemented by one of Auckland's most creative cocktail and sake programmes. Fashionable and frequently packed, Azabu is essential dining for the city's affluent social crowd.
highlights: Japanese-Peruvian Nikkei cuisine; glamorous Ponsonby setting; creative cocktail and sake programme; Savor Group quality; fashionable social dining crowd.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Concrete Playground; Time Out Auckland

description: Kingi is The Hotel Britomart's acclaimed seafood restaurant, a beautifully designed coastal dining room devoted to the finest New Zealand kaimoana — freshly landed fish, crayfish, Bluff oysters and seasonal shellfish — prepared with a refinement that celebrates the country's extraordinary marine bounty. Led by a talented kitchen team committed to sustainable sourcing, Kingi's assured cooking and impeccable hotel service make it the natural choice for business lunches and special dinners by the water. Its consistent appearance in Metro and Viva rankings confirms its status as one of Auckland's essential seafood tables.
highlights: Premier NZ seafood restaurant at Hotel Britomart; sustainable kaimoana focus; Bluff oysters; crayfish; refined coastal menu; top-50 regular.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Auckland NZ Official; Time Out Auckland

description: Cibo is a Parnell institution and one of Auckland's most beloved upscale restaurants, occupying a warmly lit room in a gracious heritage villa on St Georges Bay Road with a loyal following of well-heeled locals who have made it their restaurant of choice for decades. The Italian-influenced New Zealand menu of seasonal produce, handmade pasta and prime local proteins is executed with quiet authority, while the wine list and warm service complete a dining experience of effortless elegance. Consistently cited among Auckland's best restaurant experiences year on year, Cibo endures as a defining Parnell address.
highlights: Parnell institution; heritage villa setting; Italian-NZ seasonal menu; loyal affluent local following; excellent wine programme; decades of culinary excellence.
sources: Metro Top 50 multiple years; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; NZ Herald; Broadsheet

description: Masu is SkyCity Auckland's celebrated Japanese robata restaurant led by acclaimed chef Nic Watt, a sophisticated izakaya-style dining room where premium ingredients are grilled over binchotan charcoal with exquisite Japanese precision. The menu spans sashimi, premium wagyu, robata skewers and elaborate seasonal specials, delivered in a warmly lit, design-led room that balances formality with sociability. With an exceptional sake and Japanese whisky programme and one of the most consistent fine-dining track records in the CBD, Masu is an enduring Auckland benchmark.
highlights: Japanese robata dining at SkyCity; binchotan charcoal grilling; premium wagyu and sashimi; exceptional sake and Japanese whisky; consistently top-50 CBD restaurant; chef Nic Watt's acclaimed pedigree.
sources: Metro Top 50; Viva Top 60; Cuisine Good Food Guide; Heart of the City; Auckland NZ Official
